findings.check: ATLAS heartbeat parse (health, GI, anomalies, EVE tension)
findings.detail: Reviewed 20260327T090405Z.json: health=degraded, composite=0.668, anomalies=2, gi=0.83 mode=green, eve.global_tension=high, dominant_region=Global. Endpoints micro_agents, integrity, eve_news all true.

findings.check: Production /api/signals/micro vs ATLAS-flagged anomaly count and identity
findings.detail: Live fetch ~2026-03-27T10:02:48Z: anomalies[] still exactly two elevated signals matching ATLAS: GAIA/USGS (48 quakes M2.5+ today, max M6.5 near Mendi, PNG) and DAEDALUS-µ Self-ping HTTP 401 in 119ms. Composite 0.646 vs heartbeat 0.668 (~3% lower) — consistent with continued strain, not recovery. THEMIS block: healthy=false, mode=degraded, errors include data.gov catalog API fetch failed and no transparency fallback; sourceStatus shows Federal Register ok but data.gov paths failed — explains agent-level degradation beyond the two composite anomalies.

Both heartbeat-era anomaly types persist (not transient). Seismic remains elevated; max magnitude in this snapshot (M6.5) is higher than the prior ZEUS run’s cited sample (M5.6), so local severity of the seismic signal ticked up while still labeled elevated. Self-ping stays elevated with HTTP 401 — stable pattern, likely intentional auth on health endpoint or persistent misconfiguration.

source=mock — global_integrity=0.83 and mode=green are not production-verified. terminal_status=nominal matches heartbeat gi/mode snapshot but interpretability is limited. signals.freshness=0.6 and information=0.6 indicate degraded dimensions versus 1.0; kv=false aligns with non-KV path. Timestamp ~2026-03-27T10:02:47Z is fresh relative to this run.

findings.check: Vercel deployment health (READY, ERROR in 6h, ignoreCommand)
findings.detail: No Vercel CLI or VERCEL_TOKEN in this environment — could not list deployments or confirm READY vs ERROR in the last six hours, or observe ignoreCommand skipping bot builds. Repo vercel.json defines ignoreCommand as bash scripts/ignore-build.sh (reference only). Production HTTPS for /api/signals/micro, /api/integrity-status, and /api/eve/global-news returned 200 at verification time.

findings.check: EVE /api/eve/global-news vs GI and micro anomalies
findings.detail: global_tension=high with dominant_category=geopolitical while integrity-status reports mode=green and terminal_status=nominal — narrative disconnect, compounded because GI is mock-sourced. Feed mixes fresh Wikipedia current-events rows with older 2022-dated articles, so tension labeling may blend stale and current signals. Micro anomalies (seismic, self-ping 401) do not map cleanly to headline themes; they read as natural-hazard and infrastructure/auth signals.

eve_correlation: High global_tension aligns with geopolitical-heavy EVE items but does not explain USGS seismic elevation or Self-ping 401. Mixed feed freshness weakens strict news-to-signal correlation; persistent anomalies are better attributed to environmental and application health than to EVE headlines.
recommendation: Treat ATLAS degraded as ground-truth for operations: two persistent elevated micro signals plus THEMIS data.gov path failures. Do not treat GI green as verified until integrity-status source is live; re-run ZEUS with Vercel API access to validate deployment state and ignoreCommand behavior. Clarify whether Self-ping 401 is expected; if so, exclude or downgrade it in anomaly logic to reduce noise.
